questions.explanation: Željeti/željeti → želim = I want. Trebam = I need. Mogu = I can. Moram = I must.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'I can speak Croatian.'
questions.explanation: Mogu + infinitive. The second verb stays in the infinitive form, not conjugated.
Kruh = bread. Mlijeko = milk. Sir = cheese. Voda = water.
Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -u in the accusative: kava → kavu.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'the bill, please' in a restaurant?
questions.explanation: Račun = bill/check. Jelovnik = menu. Narudžba = order. Stol = table.
Skupo = expensive. Jeftino = cheap. Besplatno = free. Lijepo = nice/beautiful.

questions.prompt: Where do the clitic pronouns go in: '___ ___ daj.'?
questions.hint: In imperatives, clitics come after the first word.
questions.explanation: Clitic word order: clitics cluster in second position. 'Mi' and 'to' are clitics.
Želim = I want (1st person singular of željeti).
Mora = must/has to (3rd person singular of morati).
Pizza → pizzu in the accusative (feminine -a → -u).
Možemo = we can (1st person plural of moći).
Košta = costs. Koliko košta? = How much does it cost?
Riba → ribu in the accusative. Feminine -a → -u.

questions.correctAnswer: What would you like to order?
acceptableAnswers: What do you want to order?
What would you like to order
questions.explanation: Što = what. Želite = you want (formal). Naručiti = to order.
Mogu li = can I (question form). Dobiti = to get/receive. Jelovnik = menu.

questions.correctAnswer: How much does a kilogram of apples cost?
acceptableAnswers: How much is a kilo of apples?
questions.explanation: Koliko košta = how much does it cost. Kilogram = kilogram. Jabuka = apples (genitive plural).

questions.correctAnswer: I must buy milk and bread.
acceptableAnswers: I have to buy milk and bread.
I need to buy milk and bread.
questions.explanation: Moram = I must. Kupiti = to buy. Mlijeko = milk. Kruh = bread.

questions.prompt: Za mene jednu veliku pivo, molim.
questions.correctAnswer: For me one large beer, please.
acceptableAnswers: A large beer for me, please.
One large beer for me, please.
questions.explanation: Za mene = for me. Jednu veliku = one large (acc fem). Pivo = beer. Molim = please.

questions.prompt: I would like a coffee, please.
questions.explanation: Želim = I want/would like. Kava → kavu (accusative). Molim = please.
Koliko = how much. Ovo = this. Košta = costs.
Želimo = we want. Jesti = to eat. Riba → ribu (accusative).

questions.hint: Use 'li' after the verb for yes/no questions.
questions.explanation: Možete = you can (formal). Li = question particle. Mi = me (dative). Pomoći = to help.
Moram = I must. Ići = to go. Sada = now.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'željeti' (to want) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja želim, ti želiš, on/ona/ono želi, mi želimo, vi želite, oni/one/ona žele
questions.hint: This is an -im conjugation verb (despite the -jeti ending).
questions.explanation: Željeti → želim. Follows the -im pattern like voljeti and živjeti.
questions.exampleTranslation: I want to order lunch for two.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'moći' (to be able/can) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja mogu, ti možeš, on/ona/ono može, mi možemo, vi možete, oni/one/ona mogu
questions.hint: This is irregular — note the 1st singular and 3rd plural are the same!
questions.explanation: Moći is irregular. Mogu (1sg) = mogu (3pl). The stem alternates between mog- and mož-.
questions.exampleTranslation: Can you give me the bill?

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'morati' (to must/have to) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja moram, ti moraš, on/ona/ono mora, mi moramo, vi morate, oni/one/ona moraju
questions.hint: This is a standard -am conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Morati follows the regular -am pattern.
questions.exampleTranslation: We must pay before we leave.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'kupovati' (to buy/shop) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja kupujem, ti kupuješ, on/ona/ono kupuje, mi kupujemo, vi kupujete, oni/one/ona kupuju
questions.explanation: Kupovati → kupujem. Same pattern as stanovati and putovati.
questions.exampleSentence: Svaki dan kupujem svježe voće na tržnici.
questions.exampleTranslation: Every day I buy fresh fruit at the market.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'jesti' (to eat) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja jedem, ti jedeš, on/ona/ono jede, mi jedemo, vi jedete, oni/one/ona jedu
questions.hint: Irregular — the present stem is 'jed-', not 'jes-'.
questions.explanation: Jesti → jedem. The present tense stem changes to jed-.
questions.exampleTranslation: We eat at a restaurant near the sea.
